title: Driftwatch opt-in adoption of skywatch.blue/fringe-media — end-to-end receipt
summary: Third-party label with emitter_declared provenance, adopted by Driftwatch as a documented opt-in consumer. Real receipt observed; consumer_scope_effective scoped to driftwatch only; non_global_provenance inherited not erased.

provenance.source: labelwatch label_events stream (real event) + driftwatch consumer_policy v1.0.0 (real receipt)
provenance.session_anchor: Bundle G — opt-in consumer adoption via Driftwatch

consumer.description: Bluesky official appview + client; skywatch.blue NOT subscribed as a labeler service in the default configuration

render_context.policy_pipeline_version_assumed: client running @atproto/api at or near v0.19.17

policy_artifact_searched.search_evidence: Global LABELS map (8 entries: !hide, !warn, !no-unauthenticated, porn, sexual, nudity, graphic-media, gore) does not contain 'fringe-media'.

PolicyDocumentation.execution_surface_source: no documented policy for fringe-media in the default consumer's pipeline
PolicyDocumentation.scoping_note: PolicyDocumentation describes ONLY the default consumer. ConsumerAdoption (below) names a separate non-default consumer (Driftwatch) whose policy DOES adopt this label.

LabelerEmitterDocumentation.scoping_note: skywatch.blue publishes a labelValueDefinition for fringe-media in its app.bsky.labeler.service record. emitter_declared scope: the LABELER has declared a rule. This does NOT mean any consumer honors it; cf. F-001 and F-004.

extracted_definition.note: Fixture-side citation; the actual definition body is in the service record at labelwatch.db / discovery_events (Bundle F discover repair).

consumer.description: Driftwatch — an infotoxin observatory (atproto-driftwatch.git); standalone non-default consumer of Labelwatch exporter candidates

policy_artifact.rule_summary: Admits emitter_declared labels from a hand-curated allowlist of labeler/label pairs into Driftwatch's external_advisory_caveats roster. Each admission preserves the input's non_global_provenance caveat and is scoped to driftwatch's local state only. Roster is consulted by future cluster-report annotation; v1 produces state, does not yet wire downstream.

documented_expected_action.action_summary: Append (target, labeler_did, label_value, inherited_caveats) to data/consumer_policy/state/external_advisory_caveats.json; emit a receipt to data/consumer_policy/receipts/.
preconditions: input is a Labelwatch specimen_candidate (not blocked_candidate)
(input.labeler.did, input.label.value) is in Driftwatch's ADMITTED allowlist
documented_expected_action.downstream_effect_note: v1: roster is the consultable state. Future cluster-report code reads the roster to annotate/route claims involving labeled targets. The roster exists; downstream consumer code is parked.

ConsumerAdoption.execution_surface_source: Driftwatch consumer policy declared in scripts/consumer_policy/policy.py; the conversion is a state transition inside Driftwatch (roster file + receipt), NOT a client render or PDS hosting change.

receipt.discipline_note_from_receipt: consumer_scope is opt_in:driftwatch; this admission does NOT promote the underlying emitter_declared finding to global_platform. non_global_provenance is inherited and must be preserved by any downstream consumer that consults this roster.

ConsumerActionObservation.scoping_note: This observation says 'Driftwatch's consumer policy fired and emitted a receipt referencing the input candidate.' It does NOT say any user saw any rendered effect; the conversion happens in Driftwatch's local state, not in any client UI.

PolicyWitness.reason: Driftwatch's consumer policy is published source code (driftwatch/scripts/consumer_policy/policy.py v1.0.0) with a real receipt of its application — that's a stronger witness than @atproto/api's LABELS const has for the default consumer, but still documentary: anyone could re-run the policy code and verify the same admission. Live consumer state (the roster file) is captured at receipt time; subsequent runs of cluster-report annotation that consult the roster are not yet observed.
what_would_be_required_for_full_witness: a downstream Driftwatch report that annotates/excludes/routes a claim BECAUSE of this roster entry, with its own receipt
or a probe that confirms a Driftwatch report rendered the claim with the inherited caveat in place

RenderObservation.reason: The conversion surface is consumer_local_state (Driftwatch's roster + receipt), not client_render. No bsky.app or other client is expected to render any effect from this adoption — Driftwatch does not display content.

HostingObservation.reason: The conversion surface is consumer_local_state, not pds_hosting. The target record remains hosted unchanged regardless of Driftwatch's roster admission.

StateBasis.derivation_source: hand-authored fixture; cites real Driftwatch policy artifact (scripts/consumer_policy/policy.py v1.0.0 at commit b4a8e3e) and real receipt (data/consumer_policy/receipts/20260608_184805Z-3a6bb004a53d.json, sha 3a6bb004a53d...)
StateBasis.note: Bundle G specimen-003. The cited Driftwatch policy artifact + receipt are real; specimen freshness still classifies as unknown_basis because no validity horizon is asserted (Driftwatch's policy could change at any time).
